data.title: ICICI Lombard shares will react to Q4 results with premium growth ahead of industry
data.description:
Shares of ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company will be in focus on Thursday, April 16, after the company reported a steady set of Q4 FY26 numbers.

Profit after tax (PAT) rose 7.3% to ₹547 crore, compared to ₹510 crore in the same quarter last year. On a reported basis, PAT came in at ₹539 crore, up 15.6% year-on-year.

Profit before tax (PBT) increased 7.5% YoY to ₹718 crore in Q4 FY26, versus ₹668 crore a year ago. The combined ratio improved to 101.2% in Q4 FY26 from 102.5% in Q4 FY25, indicating better underwriting discipline and cost control, although it remained above the breakeven mark of 100%.

The company maintained a strong balance sheet, with a solvency ratio of 2.67x as of March 31, 2026, well above the regulatory requirement of 1.50x. However, this was marginally lower than 2.69x as of December 31, 2025, due to mark-to-market losses in its equity portfolio.

Gross Direct Premium Income (GDPI) for the quarter grew 18.2% YoY, outpacing industry growth of 10.9%, pointing to continued market share gains.

In the year-ago period, the company had reported a PAT of ₹509.59 crore. Total income rose 17.12% YoY to ₹6,618.76 crore in the March quarter of FY26, from ₹5,651.17 crore a year earlier.

The board has recommended a final dividend of ₹7 per equity share, at a 70% payout, for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming AGM. The dividend will be paid to eligible shareholders after approval.

Shares of ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company ended 4.59% higher at ₹1,864 on Wednesday.

data.title: Singapore's Non-Oil Domestic Exports Likely Gained Pace in March, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's non-oil domestic exports likely rose 10.3% on year in March, according to a Wall Street Journal poll of seven economists. That would be higher than February's 4.0% increase. NODX growth likely accelerated partly due to favorable base effects in gold exports, Nomura economists say in a note. Electronics export growth also gained pace, driven by integrated circuits, amid the global tech upcycle and artificial intelligence-led demand, they add. The data is due Friday.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Santos says Mideast impact to last for years, its shipping routes avoid chokepoints
data.description:
Australia's Santos said on Thursday the Middle East conflict has upended energy markets with impacts likely to persist for years, while noting its liquefied natural gas shipping routes are well removed from high-risk chokepoints.

The comments come as the Middle East remains on edge despite diplomatic efforts and sporadic peace talks, with fighting and heightened risks around the Strait of Hormuz - a critical artery for global oil and gas supplies - keeping markets volatile.

Separately, Santos Chair Keith Spence said he plans to retire at the conclusion of the 2027 annual general meeting, with an orderly succession process under way.

He said the appointment of a future chief executive would be a matter for his successor.

"Recent disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on global energy infrastructure have only highlighted the strategic advantage of Australian LNG for our Asia-Pacific partners," Spence said addressing investors at the company's annual general meeting.

The energy producer said first oil from its Pikka project in Alaska is "imminent" after previously flagging that output was expected to begin in the coming weeks.

Santos said 83% of its LNG portfolio is contracted over the next five years, leaving a measured volume exposed to spot markets, allowing for some flexibility as global supply tightens.

Shares in the company fell as much as 2.1% by 0124 GMT, underperforming a 0.6% fall in the broader energy sub-index .

data.title: Fletcher Building Says Most Immediate Exposure Is In Plastics From Mideast Crisis
data.description:
Fletcher Building :

  • MOST IMMEDIATE EXPOSURE IS IN PLASTICS (I.E., RESINS), WITH IPLEX NZ AND AU MOST IMPACTED FROM MIDEAST CRISIS

  • GROUP IS EXPOSED INDIRECTLY TO MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT THROUGH SUPPLY CHAINS, FREIGHT ROUTES, ENERGY COSTS, OTHERS

  • IMPACTS ON FUEL RPICES ARE BEING PARTLY MITIGATED THROUGH BULK PURCHASING, HEDGING AND PASS-THROUGH PRICING MECHANISMS

  • ACTIVITY FOCUSED ON PLANNING, WITH EXPECTATIONS THAT WORKFORCE IMPACTS FROM MIDEAST CRISIS MAY INCREASE IF CONDITIONS ESCALATE

  • OVERALL IMPACT OF MIDDLE EAST CRISIS ON FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE FOR FY26 YEAR, CANNOT BE ASCERTAINED WITH CERTAINTY AT THIS TIME

  • Q3 LIGHT BUILDING PRODUCTS VOLUMES CONTINUED POSITIVE TREND AND GENERALLY UP VERSUS Q2

  • PRICE INCREASES ACROSS DIVISIONS RANGE FROM MODEST 1–5% TO MORE SIGNIFICANT IN PLASTICS UP TO 36% AND INCLUDE FUEL SURCHARGES
